properties.remark: THE FLORIDA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED A TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN IN THE ROAD AT 6884 SOUTHWEST STATEROAD 751 IN JASPER.
A TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 90 EAST OF FALLMOUTH. THE TIME OF THE EVENT WAS BASED ON RADAR.

AN EMPLOYEE AT THE SUWANNEE RIVER VALLEY GOLF CLUB REPORTED SEVERAL TREES DOWN NEAR NOBELS FERRY JUST NORTH OF LIVE OAK. SHE ESTIMATED WINDS OF 60 MPH IN THE AREA EARLY THIS MORNING. THE TIME OF THE DAMAGE WAS BASED ON RADAR IMAGERY.

SMALL BRANCHES DOWN AND A METAL BRACKET HOLDING RAIN GAGE DAMAGED FROM THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS.

EMA DIRECTOR REPORTED SEVERAL TREES DOWN IN THE PRIDGEN AND BROXTON VICINITY DURING THE STORM. WIDESPREAD REPORTS OF 6 INCHES OF RAIN ACROSS THE COUNTY WITH AS MANY AS 17 ROADS CLOSED DURING AND AFTER THE STORM. TIME IS APPROXIMATE AND BASED ON COUNTY STORM REPORTS.

THE CLAY ELECTRIC LINE SUPERVISOR REPORTED WIDESPREAD THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE TO TREES AND POWER LINES FROM PALATKA TO GEOGETOWN. APPROXIMATELY 400 CUSOTMERS IN THE PALATKA SERVICE AREA WERE WITHOUT POWER. THE TIME OF THE EVENT WAS BASED ON DOPPLER RADAR.

EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED TORNADO DAMAGE AT A RESIDENCE NEAR SUWANNEE RIVER STATE PARK. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED...THE HOME HAS SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE...WORK BARNS WERE DESTROYED AND CHICKEN HOUSES WERE DESTROYED AND THROWN 500 YARDS. EF MAGNITUDE HAS YET TO BE DETERMINED BY THE NWS. THE TIME OF THE EVENT IS BASED ON RADAR IMAGERY.

THUNDERSTORM DAMAGE WITH A POSSIBLE TORNADO AT SUNOCO GAS STATION NEAR INTERSTATE 95 AND HIGHWAY 17 JUNCTION. WINDOWS WERE BLOWN OUT OF THE GAS STATION AND REAR WALL COLLAPSED. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN IN THE AREA ALONG WITH MINOR DAMAGE TO AREA RESIDENCES.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THERE TO BE A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN LESS THAN A QUARTER OF A MILE EAST OF THE INTERSTATE 95 AND HIGHWAY 17 JUNCTION...ALSO KNOWN AS OCEAN HIGHWAY. THE TORNADO BEGAN OVER THE LITTLE SATILLA RIVER BEFORE IMPACTING THE ROYAL OAKS SUBDIVISION WITH EF-0 DAMAGE. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED ACROSS THE HIGHWAY AND PRODUCED EF-1 DAMAGE TO THE SUNOCO GAS STATION. RESIDENCES BEHIND THE GAS STATION EXPERIENCED EF-0 DAMAGE. THE TOTAL PATH WAS A HALF A MILE LONG AND 75 YARDS WIDE.

SUWANNEE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT RECEIVED A REPORT OF DAMAGE IN OBRIAN NEAR 22205 129TH PLACE. THE INDIVIDUAL REPORTED A POSSIBLE TORNADO AT APPROXIMATELY 2200 EDT...HOWEVER...HE COULD NOT PROVIDE AN EXACT TIME. A HAY BARN HAD ITS ROOF DAMAGED AND A COW SHELTER WAS DESTROYED. A REVIEW OF RADAR DATA INDICATED AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY MOVED THROUGH THAT AREA NEAR 2225 EDT AND THAT A TORNADO EVENT WAS UNLIKELY.

THE CLAY ELECTRIC LINE SUPERVISOR REPORTED WIDESPREAD POWER LINE DAMAGE DUE TO THUNDERSTORM WINDS ACROSS NORTH AND NORTHEAST ALACHUA COUNTY. LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG COUNTY ROAD 235 AND COUNTY ROAD 231.

THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW DOWN A SMALL BARN AT A RESIDENCE OFF OF HIGHWAY 1...JUST WEST OF INTERSTATE 95. THE SMALL BARN WAS BLOWN INTO A LARGER BARN. THE COST OF DAMAGE WAS UNKNOWN. THE TIME OF THE EVENT WAS BASED ON RADAR IMAGERY.

THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW DOWN TREES ONTO POWER LINES. POWER HAD TO BE RESTORED TO AREAS ALONG AND NEAR HIGHWAY 82 AND STATE ROAD 110 ACROSS EASTERN BRANTLEY AND WESTERN GLYNN COUNTIES BY THE OKEFENOKEE RURAL ELECTRIC MEMBERSHIP CORPORATION. THE TIME OF THE EVENT WAS BASED ON DOPPLER RADAR.
PUBLIC REPORTED A FEW POWERLINES DOWN AND POWER OUTAGES ALONG 156TH PLACE ROAD IN WEIRSDALE. TIME IS ESTIMATED AND BASED ON RADAR IMAGERY.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED TREES TORN OFF 6-8 FEET ABOVE GROUND. SEVERAL ROOFS WERE ALSO TORN OFF. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

FIRE RESCUE REPORTED POWERLINES DOWN NEAR BEACH RD.
EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED 3 CHICKEN HOUSES WERE DESTROYED ALONG ALBANY ROAD.

EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED A ROOF BLOWN OFF A HOUSE NEAR LIVE OAK.

NUMEROUS TRESS REPORTED DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 203.

*** 2 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY INDICATED A MOBILE HOME DESTROYED AND 3 METAL STRUCTURES DESTROYED. TWO CONCRETE BLOCK BUILDINGS WERE ALSO DAMAGED AS WELL AS 3 WOOD FRAME STRUCTURES. DAMAGED ESTIMATED AT HIGH END EF1 WITH PATH LENGTH 2 MILES FROM AROUND 325 PM TO 330 PM.
*** 2 INJ *** EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED TREES...POWERLINES AND SEVERAL HOMES DESTROYED NEAR GEORGIA HIGHWAY 15. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG EAST LAKE DRIVE.
LARGE TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND UPROOTED FROM THUNDERSTORM WINDS ALONG EMBER ROAD. MINOR PROPERTY DAMAGE OCCURRED TO OBJECTS LEFT OUTDOORS.
LARGE TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND UPROOTED FROM THUNDERSTORM WINDS ALONG EMBER ROAD. MINOR PROPERTY DAMAGE OCCURRED TO OBJECTS LEFT OUTDOORS.
REPORTS OF SOME TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ALONG STATE ROAD 32 WEST OF NICHOLLS. TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR IMAGERY.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED A FEW POWER LINES AND A FEW TREE TOPS BLOWN DOWN BY THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS.

*** 1 INJ *** A NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF0 TOUCHDOWN AT 632 LONGFORD ROAD. ONE MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED, AND THE INHABITANT WAS INJURED. SEVERAL BARNS WERE DAMAGED AND THERE WAS SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE. THE PATH LENGTH WAS AROUND 1.6 MILES AND ORIENTED SOUTHWEST TO NORTHEAST OF THE INITIAL POSITION. THE APPROXIMATE WIDTH WAS JUST UNDER A QUARTER MILE. ADDITIONAL POST ANALYSIS MAY SUPPORT THE MAGNITUDE BEING RAISED TO AN EF1.

*** 1 INJ *** THE JESUP TORNADO HAS BEEN UPGRADED TO EF1 STATUS. POST-EVENT ANALYSIS SUPPORTS WEAK TORNADO DAMAGE AROUND 90 MPH FOR MUCH OF THE EVENT WHICH FIRST DAMAGED SHEDS AND DESTROYED POOL NETTING. THE TORNADO THEN SNAPPED AND SEVERALY DAMAGED TREES AND ALSO BLEW A SHEET METAL SHED WALL ABOUT A QUARTER MILE AWAY. AT PEAK EF1 INTENSITY AND WINDS BETWEEN 105 AND 115 MPH THE TORNADO DESTROYED A MOBILE HOME IN WHICH THE INHABITANT WAS INJURED. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 1.6 MILES AND WIDTH WAS A QUARTER MILE.
*** 1 INJ *** POSSIBLE TORNADO. ONE TRAILER DAMAGED AND ONE TRAILER COMPLETELY DESTROYED. ONE PERSON INJURED BUT AMBULATORY. EMPTY SCHOOL BUS TOSSED INTO A DITCH.
LARGE LIMBS 4 TO 6 INCHES IN DIAMETER WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG NOBLES FERRY ROAD NORTHWEST OF DOWNTOWN LIVE OAK. THE TIME OF THE EVENT WAS BASED ON DOPPLER RADAR.
